content.full_text:
FOR YOUR INFORMATION: COMMITTEE TO OPPOSE QUALIFICATION OF RECALL ELECTION FOR CHIEF JUSTICE

TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[XX] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[ ] HELEN   [ ] OTHER TED

FROM: Jeanne
DATE: 10/23/81

[XX] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION:

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [x]

CALLER Palmer Madden
REPRESENTING
PHONE NUMBER: 415 937-8000

QUESTION: (1) Why is the effort to qualify a recall election subject to campaign disclosure provisions? (2) If a group of people start collecting and spending money to oppose the qualification are they a committee? (3) When do they file? (4) If the Chief Justice spends money out of her existing committee, what are her filing requirements? Personal funds? Forms a new committee to oppose the qualification?

ANSWER:
(1) Definition of "measure" - 82043   (2) Yes - 84200(c)
(3) First report is 35-day statement; after that filing schedule determined by election date if qualifies; (4) No change in filing dates until and if she becomes a candidate by virtue of qualification of recall election, then filing schedule determined by date of election; same for personal funds used; if she forms new committee, will be subject to 84200(d) - must file 35-day statement then filing schedule determined by date of election.
